To allocate the remaining expenses among the individual participants, a cheque was issued in the name of Bhagrathi Joshi. However, the execution of this process encountered a delay due to the absence of bank accounts for any of the participants at that specific time.

Once the participants furnished their bank account details, an amount of NPR 10,120 was subsequently reimbursed into each participant's respective account. This reimbursement accurately reflects the genuine expenses incurred by each participant, tallying NPR 10,120, while excluding the NPR 7,500 already charged to the project as an advance payment. Therefore, the overall expenditure for each participant accumulates to NPR 17,620 throughout the entirety of the event.
According to policy 1500 for snacks and 1000 for lodging per day in total 2500 is provided to employee and no bill shall be compulsory according to meeting minute policy . rewrite it

As per the meeting minute policy, employees are entitled to receive a total of 2500 units of currency per day, which includes 1500 units for snacks and 1000 units for lodging expenses. It is important to note that no bill submission is mandatory for reimbursement under this policy.

According to the meeting minute policy, employees are entitled to a daily allowance of 2500 units of currency, comprising 1500 units for snacks and 1000 units for lodging expenses. This policy applies, for instance, when an employee attends a one-day training session or goes on a field visit. Additionally, it is worth noting that bill submission is not obligatory for reimbursement under this policy.

Under the meeting minute policy, employees are eligible for a daily allowance of 2500 units of currency, which covers 1500 units for snacks and 1000 units for lodging expenses. This policy applies to various scenarios such as one-day training sessions or field visits, where the employee will receive 2500 NPR without the need to submit any bills. However, it is important to highlight that we still collect and review bills and vouchers for the sake of accuracy and accountability, ensuring that all expenses are justified and supported by proper documentation.
